title: List of National Treasures of Japan (writings: Chinese books)
rows: Wang Bo collection ( 王勃集 , Ōbotsu-shū ) vol . 28
Ueno Family collection ; part of a 30 volume collection
Wang Bo collection ( 王勃集 , Ōbotsu-shū ) vol . 29 , 30

Oldest extant copy of a portion of Wang Bo 's 30 scroll collected writings : one verse from the biography section , five of six verses from the prayer section ( all vol . 29 ) and four memorial prayer verses ( vol . 30 ) ; brought from China during the Nara period and stored at Kōfuku-ji ; later owned by the painter Tomioka Tessai
One handscroll , ink on paper , 26.5 cm × 447.7 cm ( 10.4 in × 176.3 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
Spring and Autumn Annals collected commentaries ( 春秋経伝集解 , Shunjū keiden shikkai ) fragments of vol . 2
Reverse side contains a 1078 transcription by a Kōyasan monk ; manuscript returned to China during the Meiji period , returned to Japan 50 years later
Kyoto Kyoto Yurinkan Fujii Saiseikai Yurinkan Museum of Art ( 藤井斉成会有鄰館 , Fujii Saiseikai Yūrinkan ) , Kyoto
Book of Han , biography of Sima Xiangru ( 漢書楊雄伝 , Kanjo yōyūden ) vol . 57
Anthology of Emperor Huizong of Song ( 徽宗文集序 , Kisō bunshūjo )
One scroll , ink on paper , 27.4 cm × 137.0 cm ( 10.8 in × 53.9 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Agency for Cultural Affairs Agency for Cultural Affairs , Tokyo
Classic of Rites annotated edition ( 礼記子本疏義 , raiki shihonsogi ) vol . 59
Formerly in the possession of Tanaka Mitsuaki
One scroll , ink on paper , 28.5 cm × 642.5 cm ( 11.2 in × 253.0 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Waseda University Waseda University , Tokyo
Poetry anthology of members of the Hanlin Academy ( 翰林学士詩集 , Kanrin gakushi shishū )

60 four and five character verses ( shigonshi/gogonshi ) by great calligraphers working under Emperor Taizong of Tang : Chu Suiliang , Ouyang Xun , Yu Shinan ; reverse side contains a mid Heian period transcription of volume 5 of 正広智三蔵表制集
One scroll , ink on paper , 28 cm × 643 cm ( 11 in × 253 in )
Aichi Nagoya Osu Kannon Ōsu Kannon Hōshō-in ( 宝生院 ) , Nagoya , Aichi
Yupian ( 玉篇 , gyokuhen ) , fragments of vol . 9
Part of a 30 volume Chinese character dictionary
One scroll , ink on paper , 26.9 cm × 1,625.9 cm ( 10.6 in × 640.1 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Waseda University Waseda University , Tokyo
Yupian ( 玉篇 , gyokuhen ) , first half of vol . 27
Part of a 30 volume Chinese character dictionary ; one half of an extant complete volume
One scroll , ink on paper , 27.2 cm × 915.0 cm ( 10.7 in × 360.2 in )
Yupian ( 玉篇 , gyokuhen ) , second half of vol . 27
Part of a 30 volume Chinese character dictionary ; one half of an extant complete volume
Shiga Otsu Ishiyamadera Ishiyama-dera , Ōtsu , Shiga
Old-Script Document of Antiquities ( Guwen Shangshu ) ( 古文尚書 , kobun shōsho ) , vol . 6

Document of Antiquities is another name for the Book of History ; brought to Japan in Nara period ; Japanese annotations from the mid- Heian period in red and black ink ; on back of scroll : Genpisho , a document on the research of era names
One scroll , ink on paper , 26.3 cm × 328.0 cm ( 10.4 in × 129.1 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
Document of Antiquities ( Guwen Shangshu ) ( 古文尚書 , kobun shōsho ) , vol . 3 , 5 , 12
Document of Antiquities is another name for the Book of History ; on back of scroll : Genpisho , a document on the research of era names
One scroll , ink on paper , 26.8 cm × 1,138 cm ( 10.6 in × 448.0 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Toyo Bunko Tōyō Bunko , Tokyo
Shuowen Jiezi ( 説文木部残巻 , setsumon mokubu zankan , Explaining Simple and Analyzing Compound Characters ) , fragment
Chinese dictionary ; oldest extant manuscript of this work ; brought to Japan by Naitō Torajirō ; fragment concerns the 木 section header
One scroll of six pages , ink on paper , 25.4 cm × 243 cm ( 10.0 in × 95.7 in )
Osaka Osaka Takeda Science Foundation Takeda Science Foundation ( 武田科学振興財団 , Takeda Kagaku Shinkōzaidan ) , Osaka
A New Account of the Tales of the World ( 世説新書 , sesetsu shinsho ) fragments of vol . 6 ; parts of the Admonitions and warnings and Quick perception chapters

Collection of anecdotes about distinguished people of the Later Han Dynasty to the Eastern Jin Dynasty ; backside contains transcription of an esoteric Buddhist manual ; oldest extant transcription of this work ; handed down in the Kanchi-in subtemple of Tō-ji ; split in four parts ( all National Treasures ) during the Meiji period
0650 Tang Dynasty , second half of 7th to first half of 8th century
One scroll , ink on linen paper,27.1 cm × 397.0 cm ( 10.7 in × 156.3 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Kyoto National Museum Kyoto National Museum , Kyoto
A New Account of the Tales of the World ( 世説新書 , sesetsu shinsho ) fragments of vol . 6 ; Virile Vigor chapter

Collection of anecdotes about distinguished people of the Later Han Dynasty to the Eastern Jin Dynasty ; backside contains transcription of an esoteric Buddhist manual ; oldest extant transcription of this work ; handed down in the Kanchi-in subtemple of Tō-ji ; split in four parts ( all National Treasures ) during the Meiji period
0650 Tang Dynasty , second half of 7th to first half of 8th century
One scroll , ink on paper , 26.9 cm × 196.0 cm ( 10.6 in × 77.2 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
A New Account of the Tales of the World ( 世説新書 , sesetsu shinsho ) fragments of vol . 6

Collection of anecdotes about distinguished people of the Later Han Dynasty to the Eastern Jin Dynasty ; backside contains transcription of an esoteric Buddhist manual ; oldest extant transcription of this work ; handed down in the Kanchi-in subtemple of Tō-ji ; split in four parts ( all National Treasures ) during the Meiji period
0650 Tang Dynasty , second half of 7th to first half of 8th century
Tokyo Tokyo Agency for Cultural Affairs Agency for Cultural Affairs , Tokyo
A New Account of the Tales of the World ( 世説新書 , sesetsu shinsho ) fragments of vol . 6

Collection of anecdotes about distinguished people of the Later Han Dynasty to the Eastern Jin Dynasty ; backside contains transcription of an esoteric Buddhist manual ; oldest extant transcription of this work ; handed down in the Kanchi-in subtemple of Tō-ji ; split in four parts ( all National Treasures ) during the Meiji period
0650 Tang Dynasty , second half of 7th to first half of 8th century
Hyogo Itami Private private Ninishi Shinemon ( 小西新右衛門 ) , Itami , Hyōgo
Mao Shi ( 毛詩 , mōshi ) fragments of vol . 6 ; Zheng Jian edition
Tokyo Tokyo Toyo Bunko Tōyō Bunko , Tokyo

Collection of Buddhist karmic tales ; oldest extant manuscript of this work ; thought to have been brought to Japan by Engyō ( 円行 ) , a pupil of Kūkai , in 838 ; one of two Chinese collections that the Nihon Ryōiki was modelled on
Three scrolls , ink on paper , height : 28.2 cm ( 11.1 in ) , lengths : 339.3 cm ( 133.6 in ) , 557.7 cm ( 219.6 in ) and 672.7 cm ( 264.8 in ) for scrolls 1 , 2 and 3
Elegant Orchid in the Jieshi Tuning ( Jieshidiao Youlan ) ( 碣石調幽蘭 , kessekichōyūran ) vol . 5

Abridged copy of Score for the seven-stringed zither ( guqin ) ; transcription of the original by the zither player Qiugong ( also known as Ming ) from Huiji province ; in text explanation of the finger movements to perform the piece Elegant Orchid and titles of 59 pieces at the end ; original lost in China
One handscroll , ink on paper , 27.4 cm × 423.1 cm ( 10.8 in × 166.6 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo

description: This table lists National Treasures of Japan specifically related to Chinese books, providing details about their names, authors, historical context, format, and current locations. It serves as a catalog of culturally significant writings preserved in Japan, highlighting their origins and physical characteristics.
